# fenestra

> A pure-Rust native GUI framework (winit + wgpu + vello + parley + taffy)
> with web-grade aesthetics and first-class deterministic headless
> verification: humans and AI agents drive apps by semantic queries
> (`by::role(..).name("Save")`), assert on the accessibility tree and
> emitted messages, render deterministic PNGs (golden failures explain
> themselves with diff images), and script whole probes in JSON — all
> without a display server, all in ordinary tests.

The API is macro-free, fully autocompletable, and small enough to memorize:
element constructors (`row()`, `col()`, `text()`), fluent styling through
design tokens (`.p(SP4)`, `.rounded(R_MD)`, `.shadow(ShadowToken::Sm)`),
an Elm-shaped `App` trait (`update`/`view`), and a themed widget kit.
Colors route through a `Theme` generated from accent hues; flip one value
for dark mode. Rendering is a pure function of
`(element tree, theme, size, scale)` plus one retained `FrameState`.

**Verification envelope.** Headless rendering is a deterministic *subset* of
the live window: embedded fonts (Inter/Latin — the real monospace, CJK,
emoji, and RTL faces, plus the OS clipboard, appear only in a window),
reduced motion, and one reference GPU backend (macOS/Metal; Linux/lavapipe
within a wider tolerance). The full Liquid-Glass optics (backdrop blur, edge
lensing, adaptive vibrancy) are headless-only; the live single-pass window
shows the tint plus the specular rim and sheen. Trust headless for layout,
semantics, color, and most pixels; confirm non-Latin/monospace text and full
glass in a window. The web target compiles out AccessKit, the clipboard, and
the glass passes.

## Docs

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): how to author and verify fenestra UIs as an
  agent — the build-render-look loop, synthetic input, accessibility
  assertions, golden tests, vocabulary cheat sheet, pitfalls
- [README.md](README.md): overview, quickstart, gallery art, workspace map
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md): the full pipeline and every
  integration decision, recorded milestone by milestone
- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md): release history

## API reference

- [docs.rs/fenestra](https://docs.rs/fenestra): facade crate (start here)
- [docs.rs/fenestra-core](https://docs.rs/fenestra-core): element IR,
  theme, layout, text, paint, input, transitions
- [docs.rs/fenestra-shell](https://docs.rs/fenestra-shell): windowed
  runner and the headless renderer/test harness
- [docs.rs/fenestra-kit](https://docs.rs/fenestra-kit): the widget kit
- [docs.rs/fenestra-motion](https://docs.rs/fenestra-motion): frame-pure
  motion graphics — timelines sampled headlessly, temporal lints, contact
  sheets, PNG/ffmpeg sinks, the `motion` CLI

## Examples

- [counter.rs](fenestra/examples/counter.rs): the canonical Elm-shaped app
- [dashboard.rs](fenestra/examples/dashboard.rs): flagship SaaS dashboard
  (sidebar, search, table, form, modal; `-- shot` renders PNGs headlessly)
- [clock.rs](fenestra/examples/clock.rs): background-thread commands via
  the `App::init` proxy
- [toasts.rs](fenestra/examples/toasts.rs): toast stack with auto-dismiss
- [windows.rs](fenestra/examples/windows.rs): multi-window — `App::windows`
  declares the open set, `view_for(key)` routes views, state is shared
- [gallery.rs](fenestra/examples/gallery.rs): renders every widget
  headlessly to PNGs
- [lower_third.ron](fenestra-motion/examples/lower_third.ron): a motion
  composition as data — a transparent-background broadcast lower third the
  `motion` CLI renders, probes, and lints
